title: Physics for Mortals
subtitle: We unravel the mysteries of the universe
author: Wordsus Team
description: Discover the secrets of the universe in this complete guide to 365 physics lessons. From Newton's laws to quantum mechanics and the multiverse, explained in a simple way for everyone.

chapters.title: The law of inertia: why things don't stop
chapters.description: We explore Newton's first law of motion. Discover why an object stays in motion until an external force stops it.

chapters.title: Heat and energy: fundamentals of thermodynamics
chapters.description: We analyze the basic principles of heat and temperature. You will learn how thermal energy flows and affects all the materials in our environment.

chapters.title: Electromagnetism: the invisible force that moves the world
chapters.description: Discover how electricity and magnetism come together. We explore this fundamental force that lights up our cities and makes technology work today.

chapters.title: The nature of light: how photons behave
chapters.description: Is light a wave or a particle? We unravel the dual behavior of light and how photons interact with matter in the visible universe.

chapters.title: The mystery of black holes in our galaxy
chapters.description: Enter the fascinating world of black holes. We analyze what they are, how they form in space and what happens if matter crosses their horizon.

chapters.title: Quantum entanglement: particle physics
chapters.description: We explore one of the strangest phenomena in the cosmos. Learn how two particles can be instantly connected regardless of distances.

chapters.title: The Physics of Fluids: Why Airplanes Can Fly
chapters.description: We apply science to everyday life by analyzing fluids. Discover the aerodynamic principles and pressure laws that allow an airplane to rise.

chapters.title: Newton's second law: mass, force and acceleration
chapters.description: We continue with the laws of motion to understand how the mass of an object and the force applied to it directly determine its final acceleration.

chapters.title: Entropy explained: the natural disorder of the universe
chapters.description: We delve into thermodynamics to explain entropy. Understand why the universe tends toward disorder and how this defines the arrow of time today.

chapters.title: Earth's magnetic fields: our vital shield
chapters.description: We investigate the immense magnetic field that surrounds the Earth. You will learn how it is generated in the core of the planet and why it is vital for our existence.

chapters.title: The refraction of light: mirages and lenses in detail
chapters.description: We study how light changes direction when passing through different media. An analysis of how lenses, prisms and optical mirages work.

chapters.title: Special relativity: time is not the same for everyone
chapters.description: We immerse ourselves in the ideas of Albert Einstein. Discover how the speed of light alters our perception of time and space in today's universe.

chapters.title: The origin of the universe: evidence about the Big Bang
chapters.description: We travel to the beginning of everything we know. We review the scientific evidence that supports the Big Bang theory and the constant expansion of the universe.

chapters.title: Basic Acoustics: The Science Behind Sound and Music
chapters.description: We explore how sound waves propagate in air. Understand the physics that makes everything possible from a simple conversation to the best piece of music.

chapters.title: Newton's third law: the principle of action and reaction
chapters.description: We analyze why every action has an equal and opposite reaction. Everyday examples of this law, from walking to launching space rockets.

chapters.title: Conduction and convection: how heat is transferred
chapters.description: We differentiate the methods of transferring thermal energy. Discover why hot air rises and how metals conduct heat so quickly.

chapters.title: Electrical circuits: the current in our homes
chapters.description: We understand the flow of electrons in a closed circuit. We explain the difference between alternating and direct current that powers your appliances.

chapters.title: Electromagnetic spectrum: beyond visible light
chapters.description: Discover the waves that we cannot see but that we use every day. From radio waves and microwaves to X-rays and gamma radiation in space.

chapters.title: Dark matter: the great ghost of our universe
chapters.description: We investigate the mysterious substance that makes up most of the cosmos. Learn why we know it exists even though we can't see it or interact with it.

chapters.title: Schrödinger's cat: paradoxes of the quantum world
chapters.description: We analyze the famous thought experiment of quantum mechanics. Understand what superposition means and why observing something can change its final state.
